title: cracca lupinifolia
intro: Cracca lupinifolia, also known as Lupinus albus, is a leguminous plant native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the genus Cracca. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to its high content of alkaloids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Cracca lupinifolia include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Cracca lupinifolia include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and can also be used as a food source due to its edible seeds.
